expertAssessments.rationale: Standard five-question conversational AI QA covered fleet summary, server alias detail, 24h peak analysis, immediate action, and network filtering.
Playwright MCP verified rendered production behavior, screenshot evidence, console state, and browser resource calls.
GitLab tag deploy pipeline and main validate pipeline both completed successfully, and production post-deploy smoke passed.
Answers preserved concrete monitoring values and correctly reported no network servers over the threshold in the final filter query.

notes: Tag pipeline 2527097775 for v8.11.154 finished success: https://gitlab.com/skyasu2/openmanager-ai/-/pipelines/2527097775
Production /api/version returned v8.11.154, releaseTag v8.11.154, commitSha a962a6a7bd40abab57ade77e7204219c4559798a.
Production post-deploy smoke passed for /, /login, and /api/version.

Landing AI Assistant modal confirmed the current internal knowledge path is Supabase Postgres FTS / search_knowledge_text / Knowledge Retrieval Lite rather than GraphRAG, embedding, graph traversal, or separate search SaaS.

Automation session warning: after a cookie-only guest state, dashboard access required localStorage guest state alignment. This was not counted as a blocker for the AI Assistant path because the workspace loaded and all AI QA checks passed after state alignment.
Q5 response had a minor copy issue: it said to refer to top 3 network servers but did not list them. The core filter answer, no network > 70%, was correct.
